answer:
The Firecrawl MCP server is an official implementation that integrates Firecrawl's web scraping, crawling, and search capabilities into any Model Context Protocol (MCP)-compatible AI agent [1][2][3]. It allows LLMs to extract structured data, perform deep research with autonomous agents, and handle dynamic, JavaScript-rendered websites [4][5][3].

You can use the remote hosted URL or run the server locally [1][6]. To use the remote version, connect to the endpoint `https://mcp.firecrawl.dev/{FIRECRAWL_API_KEY}/v2/mcp` or use the keyless free tier at `https://mcp.firecrawl.dev/v2/mcp` [1][6]. For local execution via npx:

```bash
env FIRECRAWL_API_KEY=fc-YOUR_API_KEY npx -y firecrawl-mcp
```

This server is compatible with clients like Claude Desktop, Cursor, and Windsurf [3]. Configuration involves adding the command and your API key to your client's MCP settings file [5][3]. For more details, see the official documentation at docs.firecrawl.dev/mcp-server [1][3].
